title: Free Recall with Open-Loop Stimulation at Encoding
description: This dataset comprises intracranial electrophysiological recordings and behavioral data from a delayed free recall task with open-loop electrical brain stimulation during encoding. Participants studied visually presented word lists, performed distractor arithmetic tasks, and freely recalled words while stimulation was delivered to hippocampal and entorhinal cortex electrodes. The study involved 25 participants across multiple clinical sites, with 20 of 25 lists per session receiving open-loop stimulation on alternating word pairs.
methods_description: Intracranial electrophysiological recordings were collected during a delayed free recall task with open-loop stimulation at encoding. Stimulation was delivered to single electrodes in hippocampus and entorhinal cortex during alternating two-word blocks (6 of 12 words per list). Stimulation commenced 200 ms prior to word onset and lasted 4.6 seconds, ending 200-450 ms after the offset of the second word. Stimulation parameters included amplitude, pulse frequency, pulse width, and pulse count. Recordings were acquired using Blackrock (250 nV units) and Medtronic (0.1 µV units) systems. Monopolar recordings were referenced (typically mastoid) and bipolar recordings followed paired electrode schemes. MNI and Talairach coordinates were provided when available.
